message:
feat(pages): Add secret scanning for pre-publish security validation

Implement comprehensive secret detection to prevent accidental credential
exposure in exported Pages bundles. This adds a new wizard step and CLI
flags for automated scanning in CI pipelines.

New secret scanner module (src/pages/secret_scan.rs):
- Pattern-based detection for 15+ secret types:
  - API keys (AWS, GitHub, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
  - Private keys (RSA, EC, PGP, SSH)
  - Tokens (JWT, Bearer, OAuth)
  - Database connection strings
  - Generic high-entropy strings
- Shannon entropy analysis for detecting random tokens
- Configurable allowlist/denylist regex patterns
- Severity classification (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
- Context extraction for easier remediation
- JSON and human-readable output formats
- Wizard integration with interactive confirmation

CLI integration (src/lib.rs):
- --scan-secrets: Run secret scan without export
- --fail-on-secrets: Exit non-zero if secrets found (for CI)
- --secrets-allow: Allowlist patterns to suppress false positives
- --secrets-deny: Denylist patterns to force detection
- --json: Machine-readable output for automation

Wizard integration (src/pages/wizard.rs):
- New Step 2: Secret Scan (wizard now has 8 steps)
- Displays findings with severity badges
- Allows continue/abort decision
- Shows context around each finding
- Tracks allowlisted patterns

Test coverage (tests/secret_scan.rs):
- Pattern detection accuracy tests
- Entropy calculation tests
- Allowlist/denylist behavior tests
- CLI flag integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
